titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Cecil Harmsworth, 1 April 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941) and sent to Cecil Harmsworth (1869-1948). Magill was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921. Harmsworth was a British politician and the the 1st Baron of Harmsworth. In the letter Magill is acknowledging Harmsworth's recommendation, on the behalf of John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943), for the position of chief crown solicitor.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/14

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 1st April, 1916. Dear Mr. Harmsworth, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of 31st ultimo, and to say
that he has taken note of your recommendation of
Sir J. R. O'Connell for appointment as Chief Crown
Solicitor for Ireland, and his claims will be fully
considered, but, as you are probably aware, the
number of applicants for the post is very large. Yours sincerely, (Sd.) ARM
Under Sec
A.P.M.
1/4/16.
Cecil Harmsworth, Esq., M.P., 28, Montagu Square, W.
